description: Reliable Source Registry for VC Brain. Specification only -- sourcing/ owns implementation. See docs/SOURCES.md for the argument behind every field.
invariants: No entry may be used as a pedigree proxy. Affiliation, employer, school and investor names are FACTS to display, never SCORE INPUTS (SHARED.md Invariant #3). ONE NARROW EXCEPTION EXISTS: feature_flags.career_history_signals_enabled, default false. While it is false this invariant holds in full and behaviour is byte-identical to the pre-flag build. While it is true, self-reported career-history signals from the `linkedin` source reach scoring. Nothing else can turn them on.
Every source must yield a real observed_at from the world, not now(). If it cannot, it is ingested with integrity_flags:['date_inferred'] at the earliest defensible date (B.md).
All fetched content is UNTRUSTED. It reaches an LLM only inside <untrusted_content> via core/llm.py.
trust_weight scales CONFIDENCE IN AN OBSERVATION, never the founder's score directly.

career_history_signals_enabled.weakens_invariant: SHARED.md Invariant #3 (no pedigree) -- partially, and knowingly.
career_history_signals_enabled.what_it_does: When true, the `linkedin` source is ingestible and the three career-history rules in intelligence/flags.py are appended to the rule set. When false, sourcing/linkedin.py refuses to ingest and those rules do not exist as far as scoring is concerned -- not merely unfired, but absent from the denominator, so no founder is scored against them.
career_history_signals_enabled.why_it_exists: The product owner decided to include LinkedIn as a scoring source after being shown the objections (see the `linkedin` entry's previously_rejected_because). A stated guarantee that quietly stopped holding would be worse than one never made, so the weakening is one named, reversible, defaulted-off switch rather than a diffuse change. Turning it off restores the guarantee exactly.
career_history_signals_enabled.the_objection_on_record: Every signal behind this flag is self-reported, unverified by any third party, retroactively editable, and correlated with the coverage gaps this registry already documents. The concern is that it ranks founders with unrecognised employers -- and founders with no profile at all -- below founders with polished ones, which is the Type 6 archetype the product exists to find. tests/test_no_pedigree.py::test_career_history_rules_require_the_flag is the tripwire; docs/SOURCES.md section 6 carries the measured before/after.
career_history_signals_enabled.how_to_revert: Set value to false. There is no second switch and no other code path.

1.name: primary_artifact
1.rationale: Machine-readable API returning artifacts the founder personally produced, with a server-side timestamp we did not infer. Cheap to read, expensive to fake at volume, and the timestamp is defensible in the backtest.

2.name: corroborating_artifact
2.rationale: Real artifacts, but either the timestamp is soft, the identity link to the founder is inferential, or the platform curates/ranks in a way that biases what we see.

3.name: contextual
3.rationale: Genuine signal but heavily confounded by popularity, language, or geography. Usable to corroborate a claim, never sufficient alone to move an assessment.

4.name: enrichment_only
4.rationale: Reached through Tavily with no stable schema and usually no trustworthy publish date. Its job is FOOTPRINT DISCOVERY for founders the tier-1 APIs structurally cannot see. Findings must be promoted to a tier-1/2 artifact before they carry weight.

rejected.name: rejected
rejected.rationale: Investigated and deliberately excluded. Kept in this file so the exclusion is auditable and nobody re-adds it in hour 14.

sources.name: GitHub

access.rate_limit: 5,000 req/hr authenticated; 60 req/hr unauthenticated. GraphQL is metered separately on point cost.
access.scrape_permitted: API yes. HTML scraping is restricted -- GitHub's AUP defines scraping as automated extraction and explicitly excludes API collection from that definition. Use the API only.
access.tos_constraint: AUP forbids using collected info to send unsolicited email or to sell personal data to recruiters. This directly constrains sourcing/activate.py -- outreach must be personal and non-bulk.

observed_at.field: commit author date / release publishedAt / PR createdAt
observed_at.note: Use AUTHOR date, not committer date -- rebases rewrite committer date and would silently shift history in the backtest. Author date is attacker-settable, so a burst whose author dates precede repo creation is an INTEGRITY event, not a signal.

sources.uniquely_provides: Longitudinal proof that a person can build and sustain a real system: diff substance, test presence, review conduct, issue-response behavior, release cadence, and fork lineage. No other source shows sustained execution over time.
failure_modes: Work-for-hire and enterprise founders commit to private repos; strong builders can show near-zero public activity.
Green-square farming, vendored dependencies, and tutorial forks inflate raw counts -- substance metrics (diff entropy, test presence, file diversity) are mandatory, per B.md burst.py.
Stars are a popularity/timezone artifact and are contaminated by star-selling. Treat stars as VISIBILITY (the term we SUBTRACT in hidden-ranking), never as quality.
Account renames and deleted repos break URL stability -- pin to commit SHA and node_id, not to path.

misses: founders in defense/finance/healthcare with closed-source work
non-code founders (design, GTM, hardware, clinical)
developers in regions where Gitee or self-hosted GitLab is the norm
people who build under a pseudonym they do not want linked
bias.note: Highest-yield source AND the largest single driver of the registry's overall bias. Absence here is only meaningful when the founder's own claim implies public code.

sources.name: arXiv

access.rate_limit: One request per three seconds, single connection at a time, counted across ALL machines you control.
access.scrape_permitted: Yes for metadata. Metadata is CC0 1.0 and may be freely stored and repurposed. Do NOT rehost PDFs or source files.

observed_at.note: MUST be the v1 date, not the latest revision. Using the revision date leaks future information into the backtest -- this is the single easiest lookahead bug in the pipeline.

sources.uniquely_provides: Evidence of depth on a hard technical problem, and the co-authorship graph -- the highest-quality edge type for B's PPR because co-authorship is verified by a third party at publication time.
failure_modes: Author-name collisions are severe for common names and for transliterated names; resolve through A's resolver and keep AMBIGUOUS rather than guessing (B.md).
Author ORDER conventions differ by subfield -- do not infer contribution rank from position.
Massive-author-list papers (100+) give near-zero individual signal; damp the edge weight by 1/n.
Affiliation is present in the metadata and is a pedigree trap. Store it as a PROFILE_FACT; a test must ensure it never reaches a feature name or prompt.

misses: applied engineers who never publish
industrial researchers under publication embargo
fields that publish at conferences without arXiv preprints
practitioners outside academic-adjacent ML
bias.note: Systematically over-weights the academic ML pipeline. A dev-tools founder with no papers is entirely normal -- absence is UNKNOWN, never negative.

sources.name: Hacker News (Firebase + Algolia)

access.rate_limit: Firebase official API: 'There is currently no rate limit.' Algolia search endpoint limit is UNVERIFIED -- the docs page is a JS shell and returned no policy text. Assume a conservative self-imposed 1 req/sec and honor HTTP 429.
access.scrape_permitted: Official API is MIT-licensed and purpose-built for this. Prefer Firebase for items/users; use Algolia only for search, then re-resolve each hit through Firebase to get canonical fields.

observed_at.note: Server-assigned and not user-settable. One of the most trustworthy timestamps in the registry.

sources.uniquely_provides: The single richest SOFT-signal source we have legitimate access to. Long comment histories show, in public and at length: how someone handles being told they are wrong, whether they concede a good counterargument, whether they can explain a hard idea to a non-expert, and whether they answer users or dismiss them. Show HN threads additionally show a shipped artifact plus the founder's live response to hostile critique.
failure_modes: Overwhelmingly US/EU, English-language, and skewed to a specific ideological and professional subculture. Silence on HN says nothing about a founder.
Karma is a tenure-and-popularity artifact -- it belongs in the VISIBILITY term (subtracted), not the quality term.
Ranking is opaque and moderated; front-page presence is not a quality measure.
Account-to-person resolution is weak; many handles are unlinkable. Require a corroborating link (profile URL, matching GitHub handle) before attaching to an entity.

misses: non-English builders
founders who consider public forums a waste of time
anyone who joined after the community's growth plateau and thus has thin history
bias.note: Absence is ALWAYS unknown here. Presence is high-value. This asymmetry should be encoded, not averaged away.

sources.name: npm / PyPI / crates.io

access.rate_limit: PyPI: 'there is currently no rate limiting of PyPI APIs at the edge' due to CDN caching, but avoid thousands of requests in minutes and send a descriptive User-Agent. crates.io: max 1 request/second and a User-Agent that uniquely identifies your app with contact info -- generic client UAs are liable to be blocked; prefer the 24h db-dump over crawling. npm: explicit rate limiting is in force, handle HTTP 429; ~5M requests/month is the stated acceptable-use ceiling.
access.rate_limit_status: PyPI VERIFIED; crates.io and npm VERIFIED VIA SECONDARY SEARCH (primary pages are JS shells) -- re-confirm before running at volume
access.scrape_permitted: Yes via the documented JSON APIs. For crates.io use the database dump, not the crawler.

observed_at.note: Per-VERSION timestamps give a true release cadence series. Use the version timeline, not the package creation date.

sources.uniquely_provides: The only cheap, machine-readable evidence of USERS ACTUALLY SERVED rather than users claimed. Download counts, reverse-dependency counts, and -- most valuably -- whether the founder kept shipping patch releases after the launch spike, which is maintenance burden nobody carries for a vanity project.
failure_modes: Download counts are dominated by CI robots and mirrors; an order-of-magnitude read is defensible, a precise one is not. PyPI download stats require the BigQuery dataset, not the JSON API.
Trivially gamed at the low end (self-installs in CI). DEPENDENTS -- other people's published packages depending on yours -- is far more expensive to fake and should carry most of the weight.
Registry choice is ecosystem-dependent: a Go, Java, or C++ infra founder has no npm/PyPI footprint at all. Absence is meaningful ONLY within the matching ecosystem.

misses: Go / Java / C++ / systems founders whose distribution is source or binary
closed-source or self-hosted distribution
founders whose product is a service, not a library

sources.name: Conference talks, CFP archives and schedule APIs

access.endpoint: pretalx REST API per-instance; Sessionize JSON/XML schedule endpoints; per-conference schedule pages; YouTube channel listings
access.rate_limit: UNVERIFIED -- varies per self-hosted pretalx instance. Self-impose 1 req/sec and cache aggressively.
access.scrape_permitted: Public schedule endpoints are published for integration. Per-instance robots.txt must be checked at fetch time -- do not assume.

observed_at.note: Conference date is reliable; talk PUBLICATION (video upload) can lag by months. Use the conference date as observed_at, not the upload date.

sources.uniquely_provides: Third-party SELECTION as a proxy for peer respect, plus the single best public artifact for COMMUNICATION ABILITY -- a recorded talk shows whether someone can hold a room and explain a hard idea, which no text source evidences as directly. Regional conference circuits (FOSDEM, PyCon JP, RustFest, local meetups) also surface builders with no GitHub or HN presence.
failure_modes: CFP selection has well-documented bias toward known names, funded travel, and visa-privileged passports -- a talk is a strong positive, absence is close to meaningless.
Vendor-sponsored slots are purchased, not selected. Distinguish sponsored tracks from CFP-selected tracks or the signal inverts.
Highly fragmented; no central index. Coverage is inherently incomplete and should be reported as such.

misses: people who cannot travel or obtain visas
caregivers
non-native-English speakers at English-language conferences
anyone whose employer will not fund attendance
bias.note: This is a source where PRESENCE ADDS and ABSENCE MUST BE HARD-CODED AS UNKNOWN. Getting this wrong makes the registry a privilege detector.

sources.name: Personal and technical blogs

access.endpoint: Tavily discovery, then direct RSS/Atom fetch
access.rate_limit: n/a per-site; respect each site's robots.txt at fetch time
access.scrape_permitted: Per-site robots.txt is authoritative and must be checked and RECORDED in the fetch ledger before every fetch.

observed_at.note: Prefer the feed's published date. If only a rendered page date exists, it is editable by the author -- flag integrity_flags:['date_inferred'] and use the earliest defensible date. NEVER stamp now().

sources.uniquely_provides: The best evidence of THINKING QUALITY and scope discipline: a post-mortem describing what the author got wrong, a design doc showing considered alternatives and stated non-goals, a benchmark with a published methodology. Also the primary channel for non-English builders, who blog on regional platforms long before they appear anywhere anglophone.
failure_modes: Cheap to generate at volume with an LLM, which has largely destroyed the value of blog EXISTENCE as a signal. Only posts that reference a verifiable artifact -- a repo, a benchmark, a paper -- carry weight.
Backdating is trivial on self-hosted platforms. A blog post's date must never be the sole basis for an as_of-sensitive conclusion.
Highest injection-risk surface in the registry: a founder who knows they are being evaluated can plant instructions. sourcing/sanitize.py is non-optional here.

misses: builders who publish in closed communities (WeChat, Telegram, private Slack)
people who simply do not write

sources.name: Regional developer communities (non-anglosphere)

access.endpoint: Qiita API v2 (https://qiita.com/api/v2/); Zenn RSS; Habr; V2EX API; Juejin; Gitee; TowardsAI-style regional aggregators
access.auth: Qiita token optional but strongly recommended
access.rate_limit: Qiita v2: reported 1,000 req/hr authenticated, 60 req/hr per IP unauthenticated. Others UNVERIFIED.
access.rate_limit_status: Qiita VERIFIED VIA SECONDARY SEARCH; others UNVERIFIED
access.scrape_permitted: Qiita has a documented public API. Zenn is RSS-accessible. Gitee/Juejin/V2EX terms are UNVERIFIED -- do not crawl them at volume until someone reads the ToS.

observed_at.note: Non-Latin names must survive normalization unchanged. Silently ASCII-folding a name is the Type 6 failure mode named in B.md -- flag integrity_flags:['transliterated_name'] and keep the original.

sources.uniquely_provides: The only route to the founder this product exists to find. A builder in Tokyo, Sao Paulo, Lagos or Shenzhen may have a deep, sustained, publicly-documented body of work that produces ZERO hits on GitHub-trending, HN, and arXiv. Excluding this tier does not make the registry neutral -- it makes it a detector for Bay-Area-adjacency.
failure_modes: Requires non-English processing; naive keyword matching returns nothing and the failure is SILENT, which is the worst kind.
Coverage is thin and uneven -- we cover Japan and Russia and China partially, and Africa, South Asia, Latin America and MENA barely at all. This is stated in coverage_gaps rather than papered over.
Identity resolution across scripts is genuinely hard; keep AMBIGUOUS rather than merging on a transliteration match.

misses: most of the Global South
anywhere the primary developer community lives inside a closed messaging app
bias.note: This source is enabled specifically to REDUCE the registry's bias, not because its data quality is high. It is a deliberate quality-for-coverage trade.

sources.name: Hugging Face Hub

access.auth: HF_TOKEN strongly recommended -- anonymous limits are materially lower
access.rate_limit: Per 5-minute fixed window: anonymous 500 API / 3,000 resolver / 100 pages per IP; free user 1,000 / 5,000 / 200; PRO 2,500 / 12,000 / 400. Returns HTTP 429 with RateLimit headers.
access.scrape_permitted: Documented public API; use the huggingface_hub client which handles 429 backoff automatically.

observed_at.field: repo createdAt / commit dates in the model or dataset repo
observed_at.note: Git-backed, so the same author-vs-committer caution as GitHub applies.

sources.uniquely_provides: For AI-infra specifically, the artifact that GitHub misses: published models, datasets, evals and Spaces. Downloads and community discussion tabs give an adoption signal for ML work that has no package-registry footprint. Also the partial successor to Papers with Code's paper-to-code linkage.
failure_modes: Download counts include automated evaluation harnesses and are inflatable.
Uploading someone else's fine-tune is near-zero-cost -- weight ORIGINAL training/eval artifacts and repos with real commit history, not model card presence.
Only covers the ML slice of AI-infra; a founder building an inference runtime or a database may have no presence.

misses: non-ML infra founders
closed-weight commercial work

sources.name: Show HN (treated as a distinct surface)
sources.uniquely_provides: A dated, public LAUNCH EVENT paired with the founder's unscripted real-time response to strangers attacking their work. This pairing -- artifact plus conduct under fire, both timestamped -- exists nowhere else and is the highest-value soft-signal artifact in the whole registry.
failure_modes: Comment volume correlates with front-page luck, not quality -- normalize conduct signals by thread size or a lucky launch dominates the assessment.
Sockpuppet supportive comments are possible; weight the FOUNDER'S OWN replies, which are attributable, over crowd reaction, which is not.
Same anglosphere bias as hn.

misses: non-English launches
B2B/enterprise products with no self-serve artifact to show

sources.name: Stack Overflow / Stack Exchange

access.rate_limit: UNVERIFIED -- api.stackexchange.com could not be fetched from this environment and the figures returned by search were unsourced. Commonly cited as 300/day without a key and 10,000/day with one; TREAT AS UNVERIFIED and confirm before use.
access.scrape_permitted: API yes. Content is CC BY-SA, so quoted spans need attribution if displayed.

sources.uniquely_provides: Explanation quality on a narrow technical question, and a long-tail record of helping strangers -- a genuine soft signal for patience and pedagogy.
failure_modes: Site activity has declined sharply post-LLM; recency is weak and a founder active in 2016 tells you little about them now.
Reputation is overwhelmingly a tenure-and-topic-popularity artifact, not a skill measure -- it is a VISIBILITY term at best.
Answering questions is weakly related to founding a company; the signal-to-effort ratio is the worst of any tier-1/2 candidate.

misses: anyone who joined the industry after ~2020

sources.disabled_reason: Kept in the registry but OFF by default. The identity-resolution and fetch cost is real, the marginal signal over GitHub and HN is small, and its rate limits are UNVERIFIED. Enable only if a specific founder's footprint is otherwise empty.

sources.name: Tavily general web enrichment

access.api_note: include_domains accepts up to 300 domains, exclude_domains up to 150, max_results 0-20 (default 5). NOTE: core/search.py does not currently forward include_domains/exclude_domains -- sourcing/ must add passthrough for this registry's domain restrictions to take effect.
access.scrape_permitted: Tavily fetches on our behalf; per-target robots.txt still governs any direct follow-up fetch.

observed_at.note: Tavily results rarely carry a trustworthy publish date. Per B.md: set the earliest defensible date and flag integrity_flags:['date_inferred']. Do NOT silently stamp now() -- that poisons the backtest invisibly until H14.

sources.uniquely_provides: DISCOVERY, not evidence. Its job is to find the URL of a footprint the four APIs cannot see -- a personal site, a regional blog, a conference bio, a non-English writeup -- so that a tier-1/2 fetch can then be performed against it.
failure_modes: Ranking is opaque and popularity-weighted, reproducing the exact bias this registry is trying to escape.
Snippets are truncated and may not appear verbatim in the underlying page -- a snippet alone is NOT a citable span.
SEO-farm and LLM-generated pages are common; a result must be promoted to a real fetched document before anything is cited from it.

misses: anything behind auth
anything not indexed
low-PageRank personal sites -- i.e. exactly the invisible founder

sources.promotion_rule: A Tavily result may NEVER be the sole basis for a scored signal. It must be promoted: fetch the URL, record it in the fetch ledger, and cite the fetched document.

sources.name: LinkedIn
sources.reinstated_because: Product-owner decision, taken with the objections below on the record. This entry is NOT a retraction of the original rejection -- the substantive argument in `previously_rejected_because` still stands and is still believed to be correct. The source is reinstated as an OPT-IN, off by default, so that the claim can be measured rather than argued.
sources.previously_rejected_because: Two independent reasons, either sufficient. (1) LEGAL/ToS: automated access is expressly prohibited by robots.txt; the hiQ line of cases concerned CFAA liability for public data and did not make it contract-permissible. (2) SUBSTANTIVE, and this is the one that matters: a LinkedIn profile is a self-authored, unverified, employer-and-school-shaped document. Strip the employer brand, the school, and the title inflation -- all of which Invariant #3 forbids us from scoring -- and essentially nothing remains that is not better evidenced elsewhere. Tenure dates are the only residue, and those are self-reported. It is a pedigree proxy almost by construction, and its inclusion would systematically favor the credentialed candidate over the builder, which inverts the product's entire thesis.

access.auth: LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN (optional; no fetcher is wired to it -- see below)
access.rate_limit: n/a -- there is no automated fetcher.
access.scrape_permitted: NO, AND WE DO NOT. robots.txt states: 'The use of robots or other automated means to access LinkedIn without the express permission of LinkedIn is strictly prohibited.' /profile/, /me/, /connections*, /messaging/ are all disallowed; only LinkedInBot has Allow: /. sourcing/linkedin.py therefore ships NO scraper, NO headless browser, and NO HTTP fetcher of any kind. Reinstating the source did not reinstate a right to crawl it.
permitted_paths: USER_PASTED -- an analyst or the founder pastes a profile URL and types or confirms the fields. A human read the page under their own credentials; we stored what they told us.
OFFICIAL_API -- LinkedIn Marketing/Talent API where the org holds credentials and the member consented. The ingestion path is source-agnostic, so a token slots in without new parsing code.
FOUNDER_EXPORT -- the founder's own 'Get a copy of your data' archive, supplied by them.
access.tos_constraint: Every one of the three paths is a human handing us data they are entitled to. If a future change adds an automated fetcher, this entry is invalid and the source returns to rejected.

sources.include_domains_note: DELIBERATELY EMPTY. core/search.py builds Tavily's include_domains from enabled sources' include_domains. Listing linkedin.com here would point an automated crawler at the exact domain whose robots.txt forbids automated access, laundering the prohibition through a third party. The omission is the control.

observed_at.field: self-reported role start/end month, as entered by the human who supplied the profile
observed_at.note: There is no server-side timestamp anywhere in this source. The dates are typed by the founder and editable by them at any time, including after they learn they are being evaluated. Month granularity only. Events are ingested with integrity_flags:['date_inferred'] and 'self_reported' -- never a defensible clock, and the backtest should treat any signal resting on it as soft.

sources.freshness: whenever a human last supplied it
sources.uniquely_provides: Honestly: very little that another source does not evidence better. Its one non-duplicated field is CONTINUOUS EMPLOYMENT DURATION at organisations that publish nothing -- the closed-source founder whose work is structurally invisible to every tier-1 source. That is a real gap (see coverage_gaps) and this is a weak, self-reported patch on it. Note that intelligence/proof.py already exists for exactly that case and answers it with a challenge rather than an inference, which is the stronger remedy.

misses: Founders with no LinkedIn presence at all -- disproportionately non-Western, privacy-conscious, open-source-native, and academic founders. This is the load-bearing miss: absence here is a cultural and regional artifact, not a fact about the founder.
Founders whose employers are unknown to the reader. The self-described-scope signal is judged from founder-authored prose; an unrecognised organisation gives a grader no anchor and reads as thin.
Career paths that are not a monotonic ladder: caregiving gaps, visa-forced job changes, mandatory national service, illness, founders who left a role to build the thing we are evaluating.
Non-Anglophone profiles, which are shorter, less inflated, and use non-Latin scripts -- the transliterated_name failure mode named in B.md.
Anyone who declines to maintain a self-promotional document, which is a personality trait and not a competence trait.
bias.systematic_direction: This source penalises the same cohort the registry already admits it under-serves, so its errors are CORRELATED with the existing coverage gaps rather than independent of them. A source that misses whom you already miss does not add coverage; it deepens the hole and adds confidence on top of it.

failure_modes: SELF-AUTHORED, UNVERIFIED, ZERO ADVERSARIAL REVIEW. Unlike a Show HN thread or a GitHub issue, nobody contradicts a LinkedIn profile. It is the only source in this registry with no third party in the loop, so it is the cheapest to game -- a fabricated title costs one edit.
TITLE INFLATION IS UNBOUNDED AND UNEVENLY DISTRIBUTED. Self-promotion norms vary sharply by culture and by gender, so title-derived signal measures willingness to self-promote as much as scope held.

PEDIGREE LEAKS THROUGH PROXIES EVEN AFTER REDACTION. Employer and school names are stripped before scoring, but tenure at a large firm, a dense title ladder, and fluent self-promotional English all correlate with the same underlying advantage. Passing the banned-term grep is NOT the same as being brand-neutral, and this entry should not be read as claiming otherwise.
RETROACTIVELY EDITABLE. A founder can rewrite their entire history after learning they are being assessed, which no other source in this registry permits.

ABSENCE IS LOUD AND MUST NOT BE. Every signal here is absence:UNKNOWN and every rule is gated on the profile being present, precisely so a founder with no profile is not scored against one who has a polished one. This is the mechanism the invariant existed to prevent; the gating is the only thing standing between this source and that outcome.

sources.name: Crunchbase

access.scrape_permitted: API requires a paid licence; the free API tier was eliminated in 2025 and full access requires an Enterprise or Applications licence.

sources.rejected_because: Crunchbase's core content is funding rounds and investor names -- precisely the fields Invariant #3 bans from scoring. What remains after that redaction is a company description and a headcount estimate, both self-submitted and stale. Worse, it is structurally LAGGING: a company appears in Crunchbase after it raises, but this product's stated purpose is to find the founder BEFORE anyone has emailed them. A source whose coverage begins at the moment of institutional validation cannot contribute to pre-institutional discovery. Also now paywalled, so the cost is nonzero for a source that would be redacted down to near-nothing.

sources.name: Papers with Code
sources.rejected_because: DEAD SOURCE. Sunset by Meta in July 2025; paperswithcode.com now redirects to Hugging Face. Its most valuable feature for us -- the verified paper-to-repository link, which is exactly the 'they published it AND they built it' conjunction -- no longer has a live successor with comparable coverage. The historical dump at paperswithcode/paperswithcode-data is frozen and could serve as a STATIC BACKFILL for pre-2025 as_of windows in the backtest, but it must never be treated as current. Live substitute: Hugging Face Hub plus arXiv, which together recover part of the linkage.

sources.name: Discord / Slack community archives

access.scrape_permitted: NO for the general case. Discord's ToS prohibits unauthorized automated data collection; self-botting is a bannable offence. Slack workspace content is private to the workspace.

sources.rejected_because: Rejected on ETHICS first, legality second, quality third. Community chat is a semi-private space where people debug, vent, and ask naive questions with a reasonable expectation that it is not being mined to evaluate them as an investment. Harvesting it would be a genuine consent violation, and the reputational cost to this product if it surfaced would exceed any signal gained. Legally, automated collection breaches Discord's ToS and Slack workspace privacy. Technically, it is also the single easiest surface to astroturf. NARROW EXCEPTION worth revisiting: publicly-archived, explicitly-public mailing lists and forums where participants know the archive is public and indexed -- LKML, Apache lists, Discourse instances with open archives. Those are a different consent situation and could be added as tier 3.

sources.name: Product Hunt

access.scrape_permitted: Documented GraphQL API with a non-expiring developer token; rate limit reported as 6,250 complexity points per 15 min.
access.rate_limit_status: UNVERIFIED (secondary sources disagree: 6,250 complexity points vs 900 requests per 15 min)

sources.rejected_because: Access is fine; the SIGNAL is the problem, and it is also cut item #1 in SHARED.md. Product Hunt rank is a launch-marketing outcome -- it measures the size of the audience a founder could mobilize on one day, which correlates with existing network and audience, i.e. the visibility term we explicitly SUBTRACT in hidden-ranking. Upvotes are openly traded. For deep AI-infra and dev-tools, the relevant launch surface is Show HN or a package registry release, both of which we already cover with better timestamps and far better conduct signal. Including PH would actively pull the ranking toward the well-connected founder.

sources.name: X / Twitter

access.scrape_permitted: No free research tier; API access is paid and tiered, and scraping is prohibited by ToS.

sources.rejected_because: Cost is real and the signal is close to pure visibility. Follower counts and engagement measure audience, which is the term this product subtracts. Whatever genuine technical discourse happens there is nearly impossible to separate from performance, and the platform is the most heavily astroturfed surface considered. If a founder's technical argument matters, it will exist as a blog post, a repo, or an HN comment -- all cheaper, better-timestamped, and less gamed.

sources.name: Google Scholar

access.scrape_permitted: NO. No public API; aggressive anti-bot measures; automated access violates Google ToS.

sources.rejected_because: No API, scraping prohibited and technically blocked. Its distinctive field -- citation count -- is a fame-and-tenure metric that would fail SHARED.md's own H12 fame-vs-trajectory gate. arXiv and OpenAlex cover the underlying publication facts through legitimate channels with real v1 timestamps.

sources.name: TechCrunch

access.rate_limit: UNVERIFIED -- no published API rate limit; reached via Tavily discovery plus a single direct fetch per promoted URL. Self-impose 1 req/sec.
access.scrape_permitted: Article pages are publicly served and indexed. Fetch only URLs promoted from a search result, one at a time, with a descriptive User-Agent. Do not crawl.

observed_at.note: Publication date is when the OUTLET wrote, not when the world produced the underlying fact. It is a corroboration timestamp only and must never be used as an artifact's observed_at.

sources.uniquely_provides: Independent third-party reporting of a funding, launch or hiring event that a founder has ALSO asserted in their deck. Its only use is moving a DECK_CLAIM from NOT_ATTEMPTED to VERIFIED or CONTRADICTED in intelligence/validator.py -- the state transition the four-state validator most lacks.
sources.scoring_exclusion_rationale: As a SCORE input this is the same class as crunchbase and producthunt, both rejected below, for the same two reasons. (1) Coverage measures PR budget and prior visibility -- precisely the term graph.hidden_ranking SUBTRACTS -- so admitting it would pull the ranking toward the already-visible founder and invert the product's thesis. (2) It is lagging: it reports on a founder AFTER they have broken out, which is after the moment this product acts in. For CORROBORATION neither objection binds, because verification does not ADD score, it only removes doubt; fame bias cannot enter through a door that only subtracts uncertainty. The distinction is enforced structurally, not by convention: sourcing/research.py::Finding.from_fetch RAISES on a fetch from any source with scoring_eligible:false, so such a document cannot become an Event; intelligence/validator.py::_verdict_event stores the resulting verdict with no evidence_span, so intelligence/screen.py::_llm_axis snippets it to empty and drops it from valid_ids; and no rule in intelligence/flags.py and no kind in memory/score.py OBSERVATION_KINDS consumes a VALIDATION_RESULT. tests/test_research.py asserts all three.
failure_modes: Funding coverage is frequently sourced from the company's own press release, so it corroborates that a claim was MADE publicly, not always that it is independently true.
Absence of coverage is UNKNOWN and never MEANINGFUL -- most founders this product exists to find have none by construction.
Article text is UNTRUSTED and reaches an LLM only inside <untrusted_content>.

misses: founders with no press operation, which is the population this product targets
non-US and non-English startup ecosystems
bias.note: Enabled for corroboration ONLY. scoring_eligible:false is what makes that enforceable rather than aspirational.

signals._scale: magnitude is a rough weight in [0,1] on the contribution to a single axis, for the per-source attribution UI. absence: MEANINGFUL = its lack is itself evidence; UNKNOWN = its lack tells us nothing; CONDITIONAL = meaningful only if the predicate holds.

career_history._category_note: OPT-IN. Every signal in this category is inert unless feature_flags.career_history_signals_enabled is true. All three are SELF-REPORTED with no third party in the loop, so per the registry's standing rule on self-reported evidence they are ingested at low confidence (0.4), which widens observation noise r_t in intelligence/flags.observation() rather than pretending to precision the source does not have. Magnitudes are deliberately the lowest in the registry -- lower than peer_selection (0.3), which was itself set low for the same privilege-detector reason.
career_history.evidences: Continuous months in a single role, brand-blind. The organisation's NAME is never read -- only the duration between a start and an end date. What it is offered as evidence of: the ability to stay with one hard problem past the point where it stopped being novel, which is the same trait maintenance_after_launch measures from artifacts, and measures far better.
career_history.gaming: Free. Two date fields in a text box, editable retroactively, verified by nobody. This is the cheapest-to-fake signal in the entire registry and the magnitude reflects that, not the trait's importance.
career_history.absence: UNKNOWN, hard-coded, and enforced by rule gating rather than by convention -- the rules are skipped entirely when no profile is present, so they never enter the denominator. Not having a profile must cost a founder nothing.
career_history.critical_note: Long tenure also correlates with visa sponsorship lock-in, caregiving-constrained job mobility, and geographies with weaker labour mobility. Reading it purely as commitment misreads a constraint as a virtue.

career_history.evidences: Count of distinct role steps within one organisation over time -- brand-blind and title-string-blind. Offered as evidence that people who worked with them repeatedly chose to give them more responsibility, which is third-party judgement of a kind, but relayed to us solely by the subject.
career_history.gaming: Free, and worse than free: inventing intermediate titles is unfalsifiable, and title ladders are denser at large firms than at small ones for reasons unrelated to the person.
career_history.absence: UNKNOWN, hard-coded. A founder who joined a five-person company and stayed a generalist has no ladder to show and this must never count against them.
career_history.critical_note: The strongest confound in the category. Promotion cadence is a property of the ORGANISATION'S HR structure at least as much as the person, so this is the signal that most nearly reconstructs employer prestige after the brand name has been stripped -- which is the specific reason Invariant #3 existed.

career_history.evidences: Founder-authored prose describing what they were responsible for. Counted ONLY when the description contains a falsifiable, checkable particular -- a named system, a number with units, an artifact that exists elsewhere -- and cross-referenced against a tier-1 artifact. Unbacked prose scores zero, in keeping with the intellectual_honesty rule that an admission must be falsifiable against another artifact or not count.
career_history.gaming: Free to write, and this is the surface where inflation concentrates. The falsifiability requirement is the only thing keeping it above noise, and where the corroborating artifact exists the artifact is the better evidence anyway -- making this signal near-redundant by construction.
career_history.critical_note: This is UNTRUSTED founder-authored text and reaches an LLM only inside <untrusted_content> via the normal bus path. It is also the signal most sensitive to Anglophone self-promotion norms: the same scope described modestly in a second language scores lower than an inflated English description of less work.

technical.evidences: Can this person actually build and keep building. Measured on diff entropy, file diversity, test presence and whether commits touch real logic -- never on commit count.
technical.gaming: Trivially gamed on volume (whitespace churn, vendored deps, generated code). Expensive on substance: faking months of coherent, tested, semantically-connected changes to a system that compiles is close to the cost of just building it.
technical.absence: CONDITIONAL -- meaningful only if the founder's own claim implies public code. An infra founder claiming a shipped distributed system with no code anywhere is a red flag; a designer with no GitHub is not.

technical.evidences: Engineering maturity and willingness to do unglamorous work.
technical.gaming: Cheap to add trivial tests; expensive to maintain a suite that actually gates a real CI over time. Weight CI-gating and test-changes-alongside-logic, not test file count.
technical.absence: UNKNOWN at prototype stage; mildly SUBTRACT (0.2) if the founder claims production reliability.

technical.evidences: Genuine command of a hard problem: a paper, a benchmark with stated methodology, a from-scratch implementation of something non-obvious.
technical.gaming: LLM-generated depth-shaped prose is now cheap. Gaming becomes expensive when the claim is tied to a reproducible artifact -- runnable code, a published benchmark harness, a peer-reviewed venue.
technical.absence: UNKNOWN. Many excellent infra founders have published nothing.

technical.evidences: Shipping rhythm over months. The derivative matters more than the level.
technical.gaming: Version-bump spam is cheap; use versions that change dependent-visible behavior. Moderately expensive to fake.
technical.absence: CONDITIONAL on the artifact being a released package at all.

technical.evidences: Working on something that was hard and non-obvious BEFORE it was consensus. Best measured by comparing observed_at against when the topic became crowded.
technical.gaming: Cannot be gamed retroactively without backdating, which the observed_at discipline plus git history makes detectable. One of the most expensive signals to fake.

technical.evidences: Third-party-verified collaboration; feeds B's PPR. Damp by 1/n on author count.
technical.gaming: Requires a real collaborator to cooperate, and for arXiv a real publication. Expensive.
technical.warning: Proximity to a well-known person must feed PPR ONLY, never a direct score bump -- otherwise it becomes an investor/employer-adjacency proxy and violates Invariant #3 in spirit.

soft_business.evidences: Public, timestamped, unscripted behavior under attack: a Show HN thread where someone calls the architecture wrong, a GitHub issue where a user reports the tool ate their data. Extract the CRITIC's message and the FOUNDER'S REPLY as a paired span. Classify the reply: engages the substance / concedes / deflects / attacks the critic / silent.
soft_business.gaming: Nearly impossible to fake retroactively -- the thread is timestamped, public, and the critic is a third party who cannot be co-opted. A founder can behave well knowing they are watched, but behaving well consistently across years of threads IS the trait. This is the most game-resistant soft signal available.
soft_business.absence: UNKNOWN. Many founders have never been publicly criticized.
soft_business.negative_form: Attacking the critic, deleting the thread, or a pattern of dismissing bug reports is a genuine SUBTRACT at magnitude 0.7.

soft_business.evidences: A README that states the problem before the solution; an HN comment that answers a novice without condescension; a talk that builds intuition before formalism. Scoreable as: does the artifact define its terms, state a non-goal, and give a concrete example.
soft_business.gaming: LLM assistance makes polished writing cheap, which has genuinely degraded this signal for text. It holds up better for RECORDED TALKS and for LIVE unscripted Q&A replies, where response latency and interactivity make ghostwriting impractical. Weight live/interactive artifacts above composed ones.

soft_business.evidences: Not downloads -- DEPENDENTS. Other people's published packages that declare a dependency on yours, third-party repos importing your library, issues filed by strangers with real usage context, forks that diverge rather than mirror.
soft_business.gaming: Downloads are cheap to inflate from CI. Dependents are expensive: each one requires another human to publish a package that stakes their own build on yours. Stranger-filed issues with reproduction steps are similarly costly to manufacture at volume.
soft_business.absence: CONDITIONAL -- MEANINGFUL if the founder claims traction or users; UNKNOWN pre-launch.

soft_business.evidences: Documented NON-GOALS. Closed feature requests with a courteous stated reason. A README that says what the tool deliberately does not do. A v1.0 that is smaller than the v0.x roadmap implied.
soft_business.gaming: Hard to fake because it is evidenced by SAYING NO in public, which has a real social cost the gamer is unwilling to pay. Adding a non-goals section is cheap; a two-year issue history of consistent, reasoned refusals is not.
soft_business.negative_form: A README listing twelve unshipped roadmap items, or an issue tracker where every request is accepted, SUBTRACTS at 0.4.

soft_business.evidences: Median time-to-first-response on issues from non-collaborators; proportion of stranger-issues answered at all; tone of the answer. Time-to-response is directly computable from the API and needs no LLM judgement.
soft_business.gaming: Response time is cheap to game for a short window, expensive to sustain for years. Use a MEDIAN OVER A LONG WINDOW, not a recent sample, and the gaming cost rises sharply.
soft_business.absence: CONDITIONAL on having public issues at all.

soft_business.evidences: Can this person attract and KEEP good people. Measured as: number of distinct non-trivial outside contributors, and crucially whether they came back -- contributors with commits spanning >6 months. Merged outside PRs with review conversation shows they can delegate rather than rewrite.
soft_business.gaming: Requires other real people to donate sustained effort. Among the most expensive signals in the registry to fabricate. Sockpuppet contributors are detectable by account age and cross-repo activity.
soft_business.absence: UNKNOWN for a solo pre-seed founder -- explicitly NOT a negative.
soft_business.negative_form: A pattern of outside PRs left unreviewed for months, or closed-and-reimplemented-by-the-owner, SUBTRACTS at 0.4 -- it predicts an inability to delegate.

soft_business.evidences: A public post-mortem naming their own error. A benchmark that reports the case where their tool LOSES. A comment conceding the counterargument was right. A CHANGELOG entry saying 'this was a mistake, reverting.'
soft_business.gaming: Performative humility is cheap and increasingly common. What is expensive: a SPECIFIC, COSTLY, CHECKABLE admission -- naming the exact benchmark they lose, the exact bug they shipped. Require the admission to be falsifiable against another artifact, or do not count it.

soft_business.evidences: Semver respected; breaking changes carry a migration guide and a deprecation period. This is empathy for users made legible in a machine-readable artifact.
soft_business.gaming: Cheap to do once, and honestly this is fine -- doing it consistently IS the desired behavior, so 'gaming' it converges on the trait.
soft_business.absence: CONDITIONAL on having published a breaking release.

soft_business.evidences: A dated moment where they put a working thing in front of strangers and asked for judgement.
soft_business.gaming: Cheap to launch something trivial; the signal is in what happened next -- maintenance_after_launch and handles_criticism. Launch alone should never carry weight on its own.

soft_business.evidences: Patch releases and bug fixes landing 3, 6, 12 months AFTER the launch spike. The clearest available separator between a demo and a product.
soft_business.gaming: Requires sustained real effort against real bug reports over calendar time that cannot be compressed. Very expensive to fake -- arguably the best cost-to-signal ratio in the registry.
soft_business.absence: CONDITIONAL -- MEANINGFUL and SUBTRACTS at 0.5 if a launch exists and nothing followed it. This is the 'abandoned demo' detector.

soft_business.evidences: A third party with a reputation to protect chose to platform this person.
soft_business.gaming: Sponsored slots are purchasable -- MUST distinguish CFP-selected from sponsored, or the signal inverts.
soft_business.absence: UNKNOWN, and deliberately weighted LOW because CFP selection is visa-, funding- and network-biased. Treating its absence as negative would make this a privilege detector.

integrity_negative.evidences: A specific, checkable technical claim in the deck ('we built a distributed inference scheduler', 'we serve 10k QPS') with zero corroborating artifact across every enabled source after a genuine search attempt.
integrity_negative.gaming: n/a -- this is the anti-gaming signal.
integrity_negative.critical_note: This fires ONLY when the claim itself implies a public artifact should exist AND the search was actually attempted and returned results that were unrelated. If the search errored or returned nothing, the correct state is UNVERIFIABLE, never CONTRADICTED -- see intelligence/validator.py. Do not let a rate-limit failure become a founder penalty.

integrity_negative.evidences: A commit spike with low diff entropy, no test changes, single-file concentration, or whitespace-only churn.
integrity_negative.critical_note: Burst ALONE must never fire this -- real fast builders spike too. Requires the substance test to fail as well (B.md burst.py).

integrity_negative.evidences: Imperative-to-model phrasing, role tokens, zero-width characters, white-on-white text, base64 blobs in submitted or fetched content.
integrity_negative.critical_note: Strip, do not reject. Emit an INTEGRITY event quoting the exact offending span. Distinguish a deliberate injection in a SUBMITTED deck (severe) from boilerplate on a third-party page we happened to fetch (not the founder's fault -- do not penalize them for it).

coverage_gaps.statement: This registry systematically misses the following people. This list is a deliverable, not a disclaimer -- it should be surfaced in the UI next to any low-evidence assessment so a thin dossier reads as 'we could not see them' rather than 'they are weak'.

misses.who: Founders outside the anglosphere with no English-language footprint
misses.why: Six of nine enabled sources are English-dominant. regional_dev covers Japan, Russia and China only partially, and Africa, South Asia, Latin America and MENA barely at all.
misses.mitigation: regional_dev + Tavily multilingual queries. Genuinely partial. This is the largest known gap and it is exactly the Type 6 founder the pitch closes on.

misses.who: Founders whose work is closed-source -- defense, finance, healthcare, or any employer with an IP assignment
misses.why: Every tier-1 source reads public artifacts by construction.
misses.mitigation: None from public sources. The Proof Protocol (intelligence/proof.py) exists precisely for this case -- when evidence is absent, ISSUE A CHALLENGE rather than infer a low score.

misses.who: Non-code technical founders -- hardware, silicon, robotics, wet-lab, clinical
misses.mitigation: arXiv and conference talks partially cover hardware and silicon. Wet-lab and clinical are effectively uncovered.

misses.why: Nearly every signal rewards a long artifact history. Someone two years into building has a thin record by construction, not by weakness.
misses.mitigation: Prefer TRAJECTORY (the derivative) over LEVEL wherever a signal supports it. FounderScore already carries a trend term -- use it.

misses.who: Builders who work under a pseudonym or refuse identity linkage
misses.why: Entity resolution cannot span a deliberate identity break, and should not try to.
misses.mitigation: Keep AMBIGUOUS. Do not de-anonymize. Accept the coverage loss -- this is a correct refusal, not a bug.

misses.who: People whose community life happens inside closed messaging apps -- WeChat, Telegram, Discord, private Slack
misses.why: Deliberately not collected, on consent grounds. See the discord_slack rejection.

misses.who: Anyone with caregiving responsibilities, visa constraints, or no travel budget
misses.why: conference_talks and, to a lesser degree, sustained off-hours commit activity both encode free time and mobility as if they were merit.
misses.mitigation: peer_selection is deliberately weighted at 0.3 and its absence is hard-coded UNKNOWN. Do NOT add off-hours or weekend commit activity as a signal -- it is a proxy for having no dependents.
